summary:
This file tells the system which files to ignore when collecting or comparing project content.

What it does:
- Excludes version control files and DiffDoc’s own working files.
- Skips installed dependencies and package caches.
- Ignores build outputs, test coverage, and other generated files.
- Leaves out logs, lockfiles, and local environment settings.
- Omits common binary and media files like images, videos, fonts, and archives.

Business impact:
- Keeps reviews focused on meaningful source files instead of clutter.
- Reduces noise from generated or machine-specific files.
- Helps avoid exposing local secrets or environment settings.
- Makes comparisons faster and more reliable by skipping large, unimportant files.

Visible outcome:
- When the project is scanned, these files will not be included in the results or diffs.
